# ARIA Practices Index

Source: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/practices/

> **No ARIA is better than Bad ARIA.** https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/practices/read-me-first/

## Practices

| Practice | Description | URL |
|---|---|---|
| Landmark Regions | Use HTML sectioning elements and ARIA landmark roles to identify page organization and structure for assistive technologies | [Link](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/practices/landmark-regions/) |
| Accessible Names and Descriptions | Provide elements with accessible names and descriptions — one of the most important responsibilities for accessible web experiences | [Link](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/practices/names-and-descriptions/) |
| Keyboard Interface | Provide keyboard support for GUI components made accessible with ARIA — browsers do not provide it automatically | [Link](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/practices/keyboard-interface/) |
| Grid and Table Properties | Communicate structural and presentation characteristics (row/column count, visibility) beyond headers/rows/cells | [Link](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/practices/grid-and-table-properties/) |
| Range Widget Properties | Communicate value and limits for range widgets (slider, spinbutton, progressbar, meter, scrollbar) | [Link](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/practices/range-related-properties/) |
| Structural Roles | Convey accessibility semantics of page structures | [Link](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/practices/structural-roles/) |
| Hiding Semantics with Presentation Role | When and how to hide element semantics from assistive technologies | [Link](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/practices/hiding-semantics/) |
